test_cases.name: edge_case_existing_events_conflict
test_cases.scenario: User has existing calendar events that overlap with proposed meeting times. Skill should detect conflicts and flag them for resolution.

input.week_description: I have a client call Tuesday 2-3pm and a team meeting Wednesday 10-11am. I need 8 hours of deep work this week.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects conflicts between proposed meetings and existing_events. Validation report includes conflicts_found array documenting overlaps. Optimization phase attempts to reschedule conflicting meetings to available slots. Optimization_notes document specific moves (e.g., 'Moved client call from Tuesday 2-3pm to Tuesday 3-4pm to avoid doctor appointment'). Final optimized_schedule has no conflicts.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Elicit and Clarify Week Overview
Phase 5a: Place Fixed Commitments and Lunch

test_cases.name: edge_case_high_recovery_ratio
test_cases.scenario: User specifies high recovery_ratio (0.5) due to high-stress role. Skill should allocate substantial recovery time and validate feasibility.

input.week_description: I have 10 hours of meetings this week spread across 5 days. I need 6 hours of deep work. I'm in a high-stress role and need extra recovery time between meetings.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates recovery_allocation with 5 hours allocated (10 meeting hours × 0.5 ratio). Recovery time is distributed as 30-min breaks between meetings, 30-min end-of-day buffers, 60-min lunch, and 30-min wellness time. Validation report shows recovery_time_percentage ≥25% (5 hours / 50 total work hours). Schedule is feasible despite high meeting load due to substantial recovery allocation.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Elicit and Clarify Week Overview
Phase 4: Calculate Recovery and Buffer Time
Phase 5b: Place Deep Work, Recovery, and Flexible Meetings

test_cases.name: error_handling_overbooked_week
test_cases.scenario: User describes a week with 20 hours of meetings and 12 hours of deep work needed (overbook >150%). Skill should detect overbook condition and flag for scope negotiation.

input.week_description: I have 4 hours of meetings every day (20 hours total). I also need to complete 3 major projects requiring 12 hours of deep work. I need this all done this week.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 2 calculates overbook percentage as 150% (20 meeting hours + 12 deep work hours = 32 hours / 50 available hours). Validation report categorizes schedule as 'overloaded' with specific shortfalls documented. Phase 7 optimization attempts 2 passes but cannot achieve feasibility. Skill flags as 'requires scope negotiation' and returns best feasible schedule with optimization_notes recommending specific deferrals (e.g., 'Recommend deferring Project C to next week to free 4 hours').
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Elicit and Clarify Week Overview

test_cases.name: error_handling_insufficient_recovery
test_cases.scenario: User has 15 hours of meetings with recovery_ratio 0.1 (very low). Skill should flag insufficient recovery and recommend adjustment.

input.week_description: I have 15 hours of meetings this week and minimal recovery time available. I need to fit in 4 hours of deep work.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates recovery_allocation as 1.5 hours (15 × 0.1). Validation report shows recovery_time_percentage <10% and categorizes schedule as 'overloaded'. Optimization phase flags as 'unsustainable' and recommends increasing recovery_ratio or reducing meeting load. Optimization_notes document: 'Recovery time is 3% of work hours (unsustainable). Recommend increasing recovery_ratio to 0.33 or reducing meeting hours by 5.'
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Calculate Recovery and Buffer Time

test_cases.name: quality_gate_deep_work_protection
test_cases.scenario: User needs 10 hours of deep work with morning preference. Skill should protect deep work blocks and ensure they meet minimum 90-minute contiguity requirement.

input.week_description: I have a major project due Friday requiring 10 hours of focused deep work. I'm a morning person and can only do deep work before noon. I have 3 flexible meetings this week.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies 10-hour deep work need and creates deep_work_blocks with minimum_block_duration_hours ≥1.5 (90 minutes). Blocks are scheduled in morning slots (8am-12pm window). Time_blocked_schedule shows deep work blocks with color_code 'green' and no interruptions. Validation report shows deep_work_hours_allocated = 10 (100% of estimated need). Quality gate passes: deep work is protected and meets contiguity requirements.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Elicit and Clarify Week Overview
Phase 5b: Place Deep Work, Recovery, and Flexible Meetings

test_cases.name: quality_gate_recovery_minimum_threshold
test_cases.scenario: Skill should ensure recovery_time_percentage ≥20% or flag as overloaded. Test boundary condition at exactly 20%.

input.week_description: I have 8 hours of meetings and 12 hours of deep work this week. I need exactly 20% recovery time (4 hours).

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill calculates recovery_allocation as 4 hours (8 meeting hours × 0.5 ratio). Recovery is distributed as: 20-min breaks between meetings (≈1.5 hours), 30-min end-of-day buffers on 5 days (2.5 hours), 60-min lunch (1 hour), 30-min wellness (0.5 hours). Total recovery_time_percentage = 20% (4 hours / 50 total work hours). Validation report categorizes as 'feasible' (recovery ≥20%). Quality gate passes at boundary condition.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Calculate Recovery and Buffer Time

coverage.notes: All 8 phases covered. All 4 test categories represented (happy path 40%, edge case 25%, error handling 20%, quality gate 15%). Deep work protection and recovery thresholds thoroughly tested. Existing events conflict handling validated. Overbook detection and optimization flagging verified.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_standard_week
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates the skill's core happy path: typical week with meetings, deadlines, and clear preferences. Tests that all 8 phases execute successfully and produce complete, consistent outputs.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: End-to-end execution with standard inputs. Verifies Phase 1 captures all commitments, Phase 2 categorizes correctly, Phase 3 identifies deep work, Phase 4 calculates recovery, Phase 5a/5b place blocks without conflicts, Phase 6 validates as feasible, Phase 8 generates valid calendar output.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Missing phases, incomplete outputs, incorrect categorization, scheduling conflicts, invalid calendar format, missing assumptions documentation.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_afternoon_preference
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests that skill respects user's energy pattern and deep_work_preference input. Validates Phase 3 and Phase 5b correctly place deep work in preferred time slots.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Deep work placement logic respects user preferences. Verifies afternoon deep work blocks are scheduled in 2-5pm window, minimum contiguity is maintained, and recovery is distributed across full week.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Deep work placed in wrong time slot, preference ignored, blocks fragmented into <90-min chunks, recovery concentrated on one day.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_ics_format
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 8 calendar output generation in ICS format. Validates RFC 5545 compliance and importability into standard calendar applications.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: ICS format generation, VEVENT structure, DTSTART/DTEND formatting, SUMMARY/DESCRIPTION fields, RFC 5545 compliance, calendar application compatibility.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Invalid ICS syntax, malformed VEVENT entries, incorrect datetime format, missing required fields, RFC 5545 violations, import failures in Outlook/Google Calendar.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_minimal_input
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 1's ability to handle sparse input and document assumptions. Validates that skill gracefully handles missing information without crashing.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Assumption documentation in clarified_week_summary.assumptions_made. Verifies skill infers reasonable defaults (1-hour meeting duration, flexible timing, effort estimates) and documents them explicitly.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Null/undefined fields in clarified_week_summary, missing assumptions_made array, undocumented inferences, crashes on sparse input.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_existing_events_conflict
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 5a's handling of existing_events input and conflict detection. Validates that skill avoids double-booking and flags conflicts for resolution.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Existing events are marked as unavailable, conflicts are detected in Phase 6 validation, Phase 7 optimization reschedules conflicting meetings, optimization_notes document specific moves.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Double-bookings with existing events, conflicts not detected, no rescheduling attempted, missing conflict documentation.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_high_recovery_ratio
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 4's recovery calculation with non-default recovery_ratio. Validates that skill allocates substantial recovery time and distributes it across week.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Recovery_allocation calculation with 0.5 ratio, distribution of breaks/buffers/wellness time, recovery_time_percentage calculation, feasibility assessment with high recovery.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Incorrect recovery hour calculation, recovery not distributed across days, recovery time concentrated on one day, feasibility miscalculated.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_overbooked_week
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests error handling for overbooked weeks (Phase 2 failure mode). Validates that skill detects overbook >150%, flags for scope negotiation, and returns best feasible schedule.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Overbook percentage calculation in Phase 2, overload detection in Phase 6, optimization attempts in Phase 7, scope negotiation flag, specific deferral recommendations.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Overbook not detected, overbook percentage miscalculated, no scope negotiation flag, optimization not attempted, missing deferral recommendations.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_insufficient_recovery
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests error handling for insufficient recovery (Phase 4 failure mode). Validates that skill flags unsustainable recovery levels and recommends adjustments.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Recovery_time_percentage calculation, detection of <10% recovery, 'unsustainable' flag, recommendations to increase recovery_ratio or reduce meetings.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Insufficient recovery not detected, recovery_time_percentage miscalculated, no unsustainable flag, missing recommendations.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_deep_work_protection
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 3 and Phase 5b quality gates for deep work protection. Validates that deep work blocks meet minimum 90-minute contiguity and are placed in preferred times.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Deep work blocks have minimum_block_duration_hours ≥1.5, blocks are scheduled in morning window (8am-12pm), no fragmentation, color_code is 'green', validation report shows 100% of estimated deep work allocated.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Deep work blocks <90 minutes, blocks placed outside preferred time, fragmented blocks, missing color coding, deep work hours <80% of estimated need.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_recovery_minimum_threshold
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 6 quality gate for recovery minimum threshold. Validates that skill correctly categorizes schedule as feasible when recovery_time_percentage = 20% (boundary condition).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Recovery_time_percentage calculation at 20% boundary, feasibility categorization as 'feasible' (not 'overloaded'), distribution of recovery across breaks/buffers/lunch/wellness.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Recovery_time_percentage miscalculated, schedule incorrectly categorized as overloaded at 20%, recovery not properly distributed.

Phase 1 - Assumptions documented.gate_definition: Every field in clarified_week_summary has a value; if information is missing, an assumption is documented in assumptions_made. Assumptions are specific and testable.

Phase 2 - Overbook percentage calculated.gate_definition: Overbook percentage is calculated explicitly. If overbooked, the percentage is documented and conflicts are flagged.

Phase 3 - Deep work blocks ranked.gate_definition: Each deep work block has realistic duration estimate (minimum 1 hour, maximum 4 hours per block). Preferred times align with user's stated energy pattern. Total deep work hours are documented.

Phase 4 - Recovery distributed.gate_definition: Total allocated recovery time ≥ calculated recovery time. Recovery time is distributed across all 5 work days. Lunch block is protected.

Phase 5a - No double-bookings.gate_definition: All fixed meetings are placed without overlap. Lunch is protected on all 5 days. No conflicts with existing_events (or conflicts are documented).

Phase 5b - All deep work placed.gate_definition: No time slot is double-booked. All fixed meetings and deep work blocks are placed (or flagged as impossible to fit). Lunch is protected. Recovery time is present on all days.

Phase 6 - Feasibility categorized.gate_definition: Feasibility is categorized as feasible, overloaded, or underutilized with specific metrics. Any conflicts or shortfalls are documented with quantified impact.

Phase 7 - Optimization documented.gate_definition: optimized_schedule passes all validation checks (feasible category) OR is flagged as 'requires scope negotiation' with specific reasons. Every optimization change is documented in optimization_notes.

Phase 8 - Calendar output valid.gate_definition: Output is valid JSON or ICS (or both). All events from optimized_schedule are present. Datetimes are correctly formatted and non-overlapping. Output can be imported into standard calendar applications.

Phase 1 - Vague input.response: Adjust - ask for specific meetings, deadlines, and time estimates

Phase 2 - Overbooked week.failure_mode: Identified commitments exceed available work hours by >50% (overbook >150%)
Phase 2 - Overbooked week.response: Adjust - flag as overbooked week and ask user to prioritize or defer tasks

Phase 3 - Deep work shortfall.failure_mode: Deep work blocks cannot fit within the week
Phase 3 - Deep work shortfall.response: Adjust - reduce deep work duration estimates or defer lower-priority tasks

Phase 4 - Insufficient recovery.failure_mode: Recovery time calculation results in <10% of work hours
Phase 4 - Insufficient recovery.response: Adjust - increase recovery_ratio or reduce meeting hours

Phase 5a - Existing events conflict.response: Adjust - ask user to confirm meeting times or mark some as flexible

Phase 6 - Multiple validation failures.failure_mode: Validation fails on multiple fronts (conflicts, overload, shortfall)
Phase 6 - Multiple validation failures.response: Adjust - generate trade-off analysis showing 3 alternative schedules

Phase 7 - Optimization cannot resolve.failure_mode: Optimization cannot resolve conflicts after 2 iterations (feasibility <80%)
Phase 7 - Optimization cannot resolve.response: Adjust - flag as requires scope negotiation with specific suggestions

Phase 8 - Invalid calendar format.failure_mode: Calendar output format is invalid (JSON parse error or ICS RFC violation)
Phase 8 - Invalid calendar format.response: Retry - regenerate with stricter validation, return JSON as fallback

uncovered_critical_surfaces.surface: Phase 3 - Deep work shortfall error handling
uncovered_critical_surfaces.why_uncovered: Would require test case where deep work hours cannot fit even after optimization. Requires complex scenario with many fixed meetings and high deep work need.
uncovered_critical_surfaces.recommendation: Add test case tc_011 (error_handling_deep_work_shortfall) with 15 hours of fixed meetings and 12 hours of deep work needed in 50-hour week.

uncovered_critical_surfaces.surface: Phase 8 - Invalid calendar format error handling
uncovered_critical_surfaces.why_uncovered: Would require testing JSON schema validation failure or ICS RFC 5545 violation. Difficult to trigger without malformed data.
uncovered_critical_surfaces.recommendation: Add test case tc_012 (error_handling_invalid_calendar_format) that forces calendar generation with edge-case data causing validation failure.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_ics_format
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 8 calendar output generation in ICS format. Validates RFC 5545 compliance and importability into standard calendar applications.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: ICS format generation, VEVENT structure, DTSTART/DTEND formatting, SUMMARY/DESCRIPTION fields, RFC 5545 compliance, calendar application compatibility.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Invalid ICS syntax, malformed VEVENT entries, incorrect datetime format, missing required fields, RFC 5545 violations, import failures in Outlook/Google Calendar.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_minimal_input
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 1's ability to handle sparse input and document assumptions. Validates that skill gracefully handles missing information without crashing.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Assumption documentation in clarified_week_summary.assumptions_made. Verifies skill infers reasonable defaults (1-hour meeting duration, flexible timing, effort estimates) and documents them explicitly.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Null/undefined fields in clarified_week_summary, missing assumptions_made array, undocumented inferences, crashes on sparse input.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_existing_events_conflict
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 5a's handling of existing_events input and conflict detection. Validates that skill avoids double-booking and flags conflicts for resolution.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Existing events are marked as unavailable, conflicts are detected in Phase 6 validation, Phase 7 optimization reschedules conflicting meetings, optimization_notes document specific moves.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Double-bookings with existing events, conflicts not detected, no rescheduling attempted, missing conflict documentation.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_high_recovery_ratio
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 4's recovery calculation with non-default recovery_ratio. Validates that skill allocates substantial recovery time and distributes it across week.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Recovery_allocation calculation with 0.5 ratio, distribution of breaks/buffers/wellness time, recovery_time_percentage calculation, feasibility assessment with high recovery.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Incorrect recovery hour calculation, recovery not distributed across days, recovery time concentrated on one day, feasibility miscalculated.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_overbooked_week
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests error handling for overbooked weeks (Phase 2 failure mode). Validates that skill detects overbook >150%, flags for scope negotiation, and returns best feasible schedule.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Overbook percentage calculation in Phase 2, overload detection in Phase 6, optimization attempts in Phase 7, scope negotiation flag, specific deferral recommendations.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Overbook not detected, overbook percentage miscalculated, no scope negotiation flag, optimization not attempted, missing deferral recommendations.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_insufficient_recovery
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests error handling for insufficient recovery (Phase 4 failure mode). Validates that skill flags unsustainable recovery levels and recommends adjustments.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Recovery_time_percentage calculation, detection of <10% recovery, 'unsustainable' flag, recommendations to increase recovery_ratio or reduce meetings.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Insufficient recovery not detected, recovery_time_percentage miscalculated, no unsustainable flag, missing recommendations.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_deep_work_protection
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 3 and Phase 5b quality gates for deep work protection. Validates that deep work blocks meet minimum 90-minute contiguity and are placed in preferred times.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Deep work blocks have minimum_block_duration_hours ≥1.5, blocks are scheduled in morning window (8am-12pm), no fragmentation, color_code is 'green', validation report shows 100% of estimated deep work allocated.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Deep work blocks <90 minutes, blocks placed outside preferred time, fragmented blocks, missing color coding, deep work hours <80% of estimated need.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_recovery_minimum_threshold
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Tests Phase 6 quality gate for recovery minimum threshold. Validates that skill correctly categorizes schedule as feasible when recovery_time_percentage = 20% (boundary condition).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Recovery_time_percentage calculation at 20% boundary, feasibility categorization as 'feasible' (not 'overloaded'), distribution of recovery across breaks/buffers/lunch/wellness.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Recovery_time_percentage miscalculated, schedule incorrectly categorized as overloaded at 20%, recovery not properly distributed.

Phase 1 - Assumptions documented.gate_definition: Every field in clarified_week_summary has a value; if information is missing, an assumption is documented in assumptions_made. Assumptions are specific and testable.

Phase 2 - Overbook percentage calculated.gate_definition: Overbook percentage is calculated explicitly. If overbooked, the percentage is documented and conflicts are flagged.

Phase 3 - Deep work blocks ranked.gate_definition: Each deep work block has realistic duration estimate (minimum 1 hour, maximum 4 hours per block). Preferred times align with user's stated energy pattern. Total deep work hours are documented.

Phase 4 - Recovery distributed.gate_definition: Total allocated recovery time ≥ calculated recovery time. Recovery time is distributed across all 5 work days. Lunch block is protected.

Phase 5a - No double-bookings.gate_definition: All fixed meetings are placed without overlap. Lunch is protected on all 5 days. No conflicts with existing_events (or conflicts are documented).

Phase 5b - All deep work placed.gate_definition: No time slot is double-booked. All fixed meetings and deep work blocks are placed (or flagged as impossible to fit). Lunch is protected. Recovery time is present on all days.

Phase 6 - Feasibility categorized.gate_definition: Feasibility is categorized as feasible, overloaded, or underutilized with specific metrics. Any conflicts or shortfalls are documented with quantified impact.

Phase 7 - Optimization documented.gate_definition: optimized_schedule passes all validation checks (feasible category) OR is flagged as 'requires scope negotiation' with specific reasons. Every optimization change is documented in optimization_notes.

Phase 8 - Calendar output valid.gate_definition: Output is valid JSON or ICS (or both). All events from optimized_schedule are present. Datetimes are correctly formatted and non-overlapping. Output can be imported into standard calendar applications.

Phase 1 - Vague input.response: Adjust - ask for specific meetings, deadlines, and time estimates

Phase 2 - Overbooked week.failure_mode: Identified commitments exceed available work hours by >50% (overbook >150%)
Phase 2 - Overbooked week.response: Adjust - flag as overbooked week and ask user to prioritize or defer tasks

Phase 3 - Deep work shortfall.failure_mode: Deep work blocks cannot fit within the week
Phase 3 - Deep work shortfall.response: Adjust - reduce deep work duration estimates or defer lower-priority tasks

Phase 4 - Insufficient recovery.failure_mode: Recovery time calculation results in <10% of work hours
Phase 4 - Insufficient recovery.response: Adjust - increase recovery_ratio or reduce meeting hours

Phase 5a - Existing events conflict.response: Adjust - ask user to confirm meeting times or mark some as flexible

Phase 6 - Multiple validation failures.failure_mode: Validation fails on multiple fronts (conflicts, overload, shortfall)
Phase 6 - Multiple validation failures.response: Adjust - generate trade-off analysis showing 3 alternative schedules

Phase 7 - Optimization cannot resolve.failure_mode: Optimization cannot resolve conflicts after 2 iterations (feasibility <80%)
Phase 7 - Optimization cannot resolve.response: Adjust - flag as requires scope negotiation with specific suggestions

Phase 8 - Invalid calendar format.failure_mode: Calendar output format is invalid (JSON parse error or ICS RFC violation)
Phase 8 - Invalid calendar format.response: Retry - regenerate with stricter validation, return JSON as fallback

uncovered_critical_surfaces.surface: Phase 3 - Deep work shortfall error handling
uncovered_critical_surfaces.why_uncovered: Would require test case where deep work hours cannot fit even after optimization. Requires complex scenario with many fixed meetings and high deep work need.
uncovered_critical_surfaces.recommendation: Add test case tc_011 (error_handling_deep_work_shortfall) with 15 hours of fixed meetings and 12 hours of deep work needed in 50-hour week.

uncovered_critical_surfaces.surface: Phase 8 - Invalid calendar format error handling
uncovered_critical_surfaces.why_uncovered: Would require testing JSON schema validation failure or ICS RFC 5545 violation. Difficult to trigger without malformed data.
uncovered_critical_surfaces.recommendation: Add test case tc_012 (error_handling_invalid_calendar_format) that forces calendar generation with edge-case data causing validation failure.

chain_definition.name: skill-improvement-pipeline
chain_definition.description: Takes an audit report of a skill definition and produces an improved version with higher quality scores across all dimensions.

coverage_report.goal: Transform an audited skill definition into an improved version with higher quality scores across all dimensions (description, inputs, outputs, phases, quality gates, error handling, exit criteria).

requirements.requirement: Analyze audit report to identify quality gaps and improvement opportunities
Identify specific fixes for each quality issue
Refactor phases to reduce complexity and improve clarity
Enhance inputs to include missing real-world constraints
Enhance outputs to document assumptions and changes
Strengthen quality gates with measurable criteria
Expand error handling with specific response formats
Integrate all improvements into cohesive skill definition
Validate improvements against original audit
Generate detailed changelog documenting all changes

execution_plan.title: Skill Improvement Pipeline Execution Plan
execution_plan.description: Sequential execution with parallel optimization phases to improve skill quality from 80.7% to 91.4%+

phases.name: Analysis Phase
phases.description: Analyze audit report to identify all quality gaps, issues, and improvement opportunities across all dimensions.

phases.name: Parallel Improvement Planning
phases.description: Six independent improvement streams: fix strategy identification, phase refactoring, input enhancement, output enhancement, quality gate strengthening, and error handling expansion. All can execute simultaneously.

phases.name: Integration Phase
phases.description: Integrate all improvements from phase 2 into a cohesive, improved skill definition with proper cross-references and consistency.

phases.name: Validation & Documentation Phase
phases.description: Validate improvements against original audit and generate detailed changelog. Both can execute simultaneously.

execution_plan.parallelism_diagram: Phase 1 (1 step) → Phase 2 (6 parallel steps) → Phase 3 (1 step) → Phase 4 (2 parallel steps)
success_criteria: Quality score improves from 80.7% to 91.4% or higher
Improved skill definition is internally consistent
Changelog documents all changes with before/after comparisons

final_output.notes: This chain successfully designs an improvement pipeline for the Weekly Time Blocker skill. All required skills are conceptually available (audit-analyzer, fix-identifier, phase-refactorer, input-enhancer, output-enhancer, quality-gate-strengthener, error-handler-expander, improvement-integrator, improvement-validator, changelog-generator). The chain achieves 100% coverage of improvement goals through a 4-phase execution model: (1) analyze audit, (2) plan improvements in parallel, (3) integrate improvements, (4) validate and document. Critical path is 10 minutes; total execution with parallelism is ~13 minutes. Expected quality improvement: 80.7% → 91.4% (+10.7 percentage points).
